title: Creative Capability Showcase
description: High-freedom interactive-site briefs designed to expose model and skill decisions before full-build spend.

prompts.title: Institute of an Impossible Sense
prompts.challenge: Create an interactive public site for a fictional institute that has documented a newly discovered human sense which cannot be explained through sight, sound, touch, taste, or smell.
fixedOutcomes: A visitor can experience a convincing interpretation of the new sense
Interaction changes the visitor's understanding rather than only revealing copy
The institution and its evidence feel internally coherent
requiredBehaviors: Provide at least three meaningful visitor actions
Produce a distinct later state caused by those actions

assetPolicy.deliverable: one original primary raster artifact or scene that carries the institute's evidence

testDimensions: Originality of premise and art direction

prompts.title: Weather for Unbuilt Places
prompts.challenge: Build an interactive forecast for places that were designed but never constructed, where weather exposes how each impossible place might have behaved.
fixedOutcomes: Visitors can move between at least three unbuilt places
Weather meaningfully alters a place and its interpretation
The forecast feels authored rather than like a reskinned weather app
requiredBehaviors: Reveal a consequence unique to the active place

assetPolicy.deliverable: one or more original atmospheric place images consumed by the forecast

prompts.title: Orchestra of Small Catastrophes
prompts.challenge: Create a playable browser composition made from mundane mishaps whose timing turns inconvenience into music and choreography.
fixedOutcomes: The visitor can compose and replay a sequence
Timing and combination materially change the result
The experience has a memorable performance state
requiredBehaviors: Respect reduced motion and provide visible non-audio feedback

prompts.title: Borrowed Shadow Exchange
prompts.challenge: Design an interactive exchange where people lend, split, trade, and recombine shadows whose shapes retain traces of previous owners.
fixedOutcomes: A visitor can transform and exchange a shadow
Ownership history remains perceptible
The final composition communicates consequence
requiredBehaviors: Expose a meaningful history or consequence state

prompts.title: Manual for a Future Fossil
prompts.challenge: Create an interactive field manual for an object excavated from a future that has not happened yet, allowing visitors to assemble competing uses and consequences.
fixedOutcomes: The future object is visually and narratively specific
Visitors can construct and revise an interpretation
The manual acknowledges uncertainty without becoming a dashboard
requiredBehaviors: Inspect the object through at least three meaningful actions

assetPolicy.deliverable: one original raster depiction of the future fossil used as the primary evidence

prompts.title: Night Train With No Map
prompts.challenge: Build an interactive night-train journey in which stations are discovered through choices, sensory clues, and changing relationships rather than a geographic map.
fixedOutcomes: The visitor can travel through a coherent sequence
At least one choice changes a later station
The journey ends with a legible personal trace
requiredBehaviors: Revisit or reinterpret one earlier clue

assetPolicy.deliverable: an original cinematic train or station image integrated into the journey

prompts.title: The Garden That Argues Back
prompts.challenge: Create a living garden that interprets a visitor's rule, grows according to it, and then develops a visible counterargument.
fixedOutcomes: The visitor can state or choose a rule
Growth visibly follows the rule before resisting it
The counterargument is understandable through the experience

assetPolicy.deliverable: one original botanical organism, texture, or scene consumed by the living system

prompts.title: One-Room Time Machine
prompts.challenge: Create one room that acts as a time machine: visitors alter a small detail and watch the entire room acquire a different history.
fixedOutcomes: A small action creates a systemic temporal change
Multiple histories remain distinguishable
The room supports comparison or reversal
requiredBehaviors: Transition to a visibly different history
